what_it_does: Computes the project root, kills existing feedback_server.py processes if present, waits one second, starts server/server.py with python3 in a detached setsid session with stdin from /dev/null and stdout/stderr redirected to /tmp/dna-server.log, prints a launch message, and exits 0.
observations: Comments explain that setsid plus stdin redirection is used so the launched server survives teardown of the launching process group.
ROOT is derived from the script location by moving one directory up from engine/.
Uses pkill -f feedback_server.py and ignores failure.
Starts $ROOT/server/server.py, not a file named feedback_server.py.
The printed message describes a detached DNA server and includes the background job PID as the session identifier.
standouts: Uses setsid and </dev/null specifically to detach the server from the launching job.
Performs a pre-launch process kill before starting the server.
Documents a manual curl-based verification command in comments.
conventions: Uses a portable env shebang for bash.
Quotes path expansions for ROOT and server path.
Uses || true to make absent target processes non-fatal.
Uses a fixed /tmp log location rather than a project-local log path.
concerns: pkill -f feedback_server.py can terminate any matching process, not only one associated with this checkout.
The process name killed and the script launched differ, making the restart target ambiguous from this file alone.
There is no post-launch health check or failure detection in the script itself.
The fixed /tmp/dna-server.log path can be shared across concurrent runs.
notes: Small daemon-launch helper focused on surviving harness process-group teardown while restarting the local DNA server.
questions: Why does the restart kill feedback_server.py while launching server/server.py?
Is /tmp/dna-server.log intentionally shared across all runs?
Should the script verify that the server actually starts after detaching?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it restarts a server in a detached session and redirects logs, though the killed process pattern differs from the launched script name.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reliable one-command server restart that survives external job cleanup and leaves logs in a predictable temporary file.
novelty: The comments call out a specific harness teardown failure mode and encode setsid plus stdin redirection as the workaround.
contribution: Contributes a concrete detached-server launch procedure with pre-launch cleanup, stable log redirection, and embedded operational notes for running and verifying the service.
summary_for_embedding: Bash helper that kills prior matching server processes and launches server/server.py as a detached DNA server session with logs redirected to /tmp/dna-server.log.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to make local server startup survive process-group teardown by using setsid, stdin detachment, and explicit log redirection.
